ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302050 represents the base churro food item. This record is a party combo of 4 churros served as a restaurant set; closest_parent used as no combo-set code exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW represents churros as a fried dough pastry category. The party combo format is not separately coded; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for churros or fried dough pastry combo sets.

serving.natural_language_portion: One party combo consists of 4 individual churros, each approximately 100 g, totalling approximately 400 g for a group of four people.

nutrient_source.title: Churros, plain (fried dough pastry) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #18617
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for plain churros (fried dough pastry, wheat flour base, deep-fried, sugar-coated). The party combo format is a serving-size construct; per-100g values reflect the churro itself. Values are consistent with USDA SR Legacy entry for fried dough pastry. Minor adaptation applied to align with typical churro sugar coating; core macros are within expected range for this food category.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for churros in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 74 is estimated by analogy with deep-fried wheat dough pastries (e.g., doughnuts GI ~76, fried dough GI ~74) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) and the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (74 × 197.5) / 100 ≈ 146.1, where carbohydrate per 400 g serving = 49.5 × 4 = 198 g (rounded to 197.5 g for calculation).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and high energy density
hypertension.guidance: Each churro (approx. 100 g) contains approximately 280 mg sodium; consuming a full party combo of 4 churros contributes approximately 1,120 mg sodium, representing nearly half the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,300 mg, and individuals with hypertension should limit portion size accordingly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index and high refined carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Churros have a high GI of approximately 74 and a very high glycemic load per party combo serving (~146), making them unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion should be strictly limited and paired with low-GI foods to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High fat content from deep-frying and energy density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Churros are deep-fried in oil, contributing approximately 19 g fat per 100 g including 2.8 g saturated fat; regular consumption of the full party combo (400 g, ~76 g total fat) is inconsistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns and should be an occasional treat for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Churros Party Combo for 4 is a restaurant sharing set consisting of four individual churros, a traditional Spanish fried dough pastry, served by Churros Republic at Funan Mall in Singapore. Each churro is made from a choux-style wheat flour dough piped through a star-shaped nozzle and deep-fried until golden and crisp on the outside, with a soft, slightly chewy interior. The churros are typically coated in granulated sugar and may be served with dipping sauces such as chocolate or caramel. Per 100 g, churros provide approximately 387 kcal, 4.8 g protein, 19 g fat, and 49.5 g carbohydrates, with 12 g sugar and 1.5 g dietary fibre. The high glycemic index (~74) and very high glycemic load of the full combo (~146) make this a high-impact food for blood glucose management. The sodium content of approximately 280 mg per 100 g is moderate but accumulates significantly across a full serving. Churros are a popular dessert and snack food in Singapore's Spanish-themed dessert cafes and are commonly consumed as a social sharing food. They are vegetarian, vegan, and halal-friendly but contain gluten. Clinically, churros are a high-calorie, high-GI, high-fat dessert that should be consumed occasionally and in controlled portions by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: No exact churros entry was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for plain fried dough pastry (churros category), which is the closest approved primary source. GI is estimated by analogy with deep-fried wheat dough pastries from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is medium because the exact restaurant formulation (Churros Republic) may differ from the USDA reference values in fat absorption, sugar coating quantity, and dipping sauce composition.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of churro (plain, fried, sugar-coated) adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. The party combo serving size is 400 g (4 × ~100 g churros). 2. Dipping sauces (chocolate, caramel) that may accompany the combo are not included in the per-100g values as they are optional and variable. 3. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for plain fried dough pastry using vegetable oil — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 4. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0; churros made without eggs or dairy contain no cholesterol — naturally zero for egg-free and dairy-free formulation. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as churros contain no vitamin C-bearing ingredients. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 7. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 8. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported; churros contain no animal products and vitamin B12 is naturally zero by formulation, but reported as -1 to indicate not measured in source. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 10. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 11. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 12. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 13. GI of 74 is an analogous estimate based on deep-fried wheat dough pastries from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. 14. Multilingual names provided for Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as this food is served in Singapore context. 15. Food name cleaned from promotional emoji format ('🔥Churros Party Combo for 4!🔥') to canonical English form. 16. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are reported from USDA FoodData Central for fried dough pastry using vegetable oil.
